text: Abstract Consensus protocols for multi-agent systems (MASs) with general linear dynamic modes and directed information exchange topologies are investigated in this paper. Most of the consensus results in the control of MASs are established by using the smallest real part of the nonzero eigenvalues of the Laplacian matrix of the network graph, which is a global quantity that needs global information of the network, and thus is not distributive. In this paper, a distributive adaptive consensus protocol is introduced. The protocol only uses information related to the dynamic modes of individual agents and their neighbors' states and as a result, leader-follower consensus can be established for any directed graph having a spanning tree from the leader to other followers. Extensions to the multi-leader containment control are then developed.
